primary_reason: The project combines community and clinical recruitment, asymptomatic controls, multiple molecular assays, follow-up severity measurement, preference experiments, and economic modeling to improve enteric-disease ascertainment and vaccine decisions.

The study combines hospitalization data, literature-derived attribution fractions, underdiagnosis and underreporting adjustments, and Monte Carlo uncertainty to estimate pathogen-specific foodborne morbidity and mortality.
This review concerns traumatic brain injury mechanisms and does not identify a concrete route for transferring its content to infectious-disease epidemiology.
The study uses national insurance claims, diagnosis and prescription criteria, population extrapolation, and coding correction factors to estimate Lyme disease incidence beyond routine surveillance.
This population survey estimates road-traffic injuries and supplies no concrete infectious-disease application or transfer route.

The linked population cohort evaluates postpartum parental pertussis vaccination using infant notifications, time-to-event analysis, confounder adjustment, and sensitivity analyses for incomplete vaccination reporting.
The review narrates dengue emergence and possible surveillance artifacts in Bangladesh without developing or evaluating a reusable measurement or modeling method.
The study links communicable-disease notifications to hospitalization records to recover missing Indigenous status and quantifies how linkage changes stratified notification rates.
The study uses capture-recapture across notification, hospitalization, and laboratory sources to estimate meningococcal under-ascertainment and assess vaccination impact and preventable cases.
This single chancroid case documents diagnosis and possible reemergence but does not contribute a transferable epidemiologic design or measurement method.
The cohort reports prosthetic-joint infection incidence, microbiology, and outcomes using standard comparisons without an identifiable reusable surveillance or measurement contribution.
The multicenter cohort combines patient interviews, medical records, payer and societal costing, national underreporting adjustments, and probabilistic sensitivity analysis to estimate dengue burden.
The report compares pediatric and adult dengue case counts and mortality over time without presenting a reusable estimation, surveillance-adjustment, or modeling method.

The study evaluates a mandatory latent-tuberculosis reporting system and uses observed ascertainment gaps to compare passive reporting with targeted, active, and laboratory-based surveillance alternatives.

The study prospectively captures hepatocellular carcinoma through multiple clinical sources and compares it with registry data, a completeness-assessment design transferable to infection-related disease registries.

The multicountry assessment uses facility record review and provider interviews outside national tuberculosis programs to quantify unreported pediatric cases and their effect on burden and medicine-demand estimates.
The study calibrates a discrete-time compartmental HIV transmission model to surveillance data to estimate underreporting and forecast reported and unreported infections.
The study applies a multi-pathogen quantitative microbial risk-assessment model to attribute gastrointestinal illness to private wells and small water systems when trial data are unavailable.
This field note identifies structural causes of pediatric tuberculosis underdiagnosis and underreporting and provides concrete service-integration and case-detection solutions.
Qualitative reinterviews diagnose linguistic and conceptual misclassification of anal intercourse in an HIV trial and prescribe clearer methods for collecting sensitive behavioral data.
The review summarizes livestock Chlamydia pecorum prevalence, manifestations, diagnosis, and control without substantively comparing or developing epidemiologic methods.
The study retests laboratory-negative dengue-like illnesses for leptospirosis and uses the cross-diagnostic yield to estimate cases missed by routine surveillance.
The study integrates scoping review, systematic review, meta-analysis, evidence mapping, and summary cards to rapidly synthesize microbial-hazard evidence for international food-safety guidance.

The study compares laboratory and clinical polio definitions in conflict-affected Syria, demonstrates systematic missed cases, and recommends supplementing laboratory surveillance with clinical criteria.
The study triangulates a century of clinical notifications with laboratory surveillance and testing practices to show how sampling sites and diagnostic capacity shape recorded gonorrhea incidence.
This cross-sectional survey describes needlestick reporting behavior and associated factors using standard regression without a reusable surveillance-measurement contribution.
The study constructs a natural-history DALY model for tick-borne encephalitis, corrects under-ascertainment and underreporting, and propagates parameter uncertainty through sensitivity analyses.
This overview summarizes the biology, diagnosis, treatment, and control of porcine sarcoptic mange without a substantive methods contribution.
The review describes larva migrans manifestations, risk factors, and control needs in India without developing or comparing an epidemiologic method.
This broad pharmacovigilance commentary lists evolving areas of vigilance but does not provide enough infectious-disease method content for a methods record.

The study uses Bayesian multiparameter evidence synthesis to combine serology, household symptom probabilities, population data, and notifications into age-specific pertussis incidence and underreporting estimates.
The longitudinal study repeatedly pairs self-reported sexual debut with genotype-specific HPV testing, using biological incidence to reveal probable behavioral underreporting.
The survey applies the item-count technique to estimate legally and socially sensitive underreporting and sale of foot-and-mouth-disease-infected animals.
The prison survey describes reported HIV risk behaviors and access to prevention without a reusable measurement or analytic contribution.

The cross-trial audit demonstrates that secondary surgical-site-infection endpoints receive less rigorous definitions and ascertainment than primary endpoints and recommends reporting-guideline changes.
The study compares reported dengue and travel counts to describe importation patterns but does not supply a distinct transferable correction or risk-estimation method.
The analysis triangulates notifications, hospitalizations, and laboratory surveillance and applies serogroup proportions to estimate missed and vaccine-preventable meningococcal disease.
The study adapts and explains a surveillance-based method for estimating undiagnosed HIV from simultaneous HIV/AIDS diagnoses, CD4 distributions, progression rates, and reporting corrections.
The study uses the placebo arm of a multicountry vaccine trial as active surveillance and compares its confirmed dengue incidence with matched national passive-surveillance rates.
The audit measures hepatitis C reporting completeness, timeliness, false reporting, laboratory-result capture, and agreement between clinical diagnosis and notification classification.
This chapter summarizes group A streptococcal burden estimates and data gaps without specifying a new or substantively compared estimation method.

The Global Burden of Disease analysis combines vital registration, verbal autopsy, surveillance, ensemble mortality modeling, and reporting expansion factors to estimate dengue incidence, mortality, and disability.
The active pharmacovigilance program demonstrates an enhanced reporting and adjudication design that is directly transferable to monitoring adverse events from vaccines and anti-infective medicines.
The study validates real-time PCR against culture and postmortem diagnoses, quantifying the meningococcal cases missed by culture-based surveillance.
The malaria-elimination assessment combines passive reports, fever testing, active case detection, underreporting surveys, sentinel sites, migrant screening, and vector surveillance.
The study couples passive submission of human-attached ticks, multi-pathogen PCR, and follow-up interviews to relate vector infection, exposure circumstances, and subsequent illness.
The student survey reports the frequency and stated reasons for unreported blood exposures without developing or evaluating a transferable correction method.
The longitudinal evaluation compares community cellphone syndromic alerts with traditional sentinel surveillance to assess rapid Ebola case and mortality reporting.
The study uses residual-serum HPV antibodies to validate administrative vaccination coverage and identify possible age-specific under-notification or partial-dose effects.
The study combines maximum-entropy ecological niche modeling with time-dependent reproduction-number estimation to map lumpy-skin-disease risk, epidemic progression, and possible underreporting.
The randomized field trial evaluates whether a low-cost telephone-beep system increases community reporting of adverse events following immunization.
The evaluation exploits staggered state adoption as a natural experiment to estimate illnesses and costs averted by the PulseNet molecular foodborne-disease surveillance network.
The study calibrates a dynamic mechanistic influenza-transmission model to underreported surveillance data to reconstruct infections and vaccination timing during the Norwegian pandemic.
This cross-sectional survey describes needlestick prevalence, circumstances, and reporting behavior without a distinct transferable epidemiologic method.
The survey describes occupational blood-exposure reporting and postexposure-prophylaxis use without developing or validating a reusable measurement strategy.
The study derives race- and year-specific denominators from hospital births to reduce administrative race misclassification when estimating infection-related hospitalization disparities.
The multicenter questionnaire describes postexposure practices and impacts among nurses but offers no evaluated reporting or correction method.
The study combines participatory appraisal, qualitative interviews, value-chain analysis, and information-network analysis to explain and improve passive avian-influenza reporting.
The study applies pathogen-specific natural-history models and corrected surveillance incidence to produce comparable DALY estimates for 32 infectious diseases.
This antibiotic-associated psychosis case report documents an adverse event but does not develop a transferable epidemiologic or pharmacovigilance method.
The prospective outbreak report describes calicivirus incidence, settings, and genotypes without an identifiable methodological contribution beyond standard outbreak surveillance.
The before-and-after evaluation tests whether an intensified management policy increases reporting of percutaneous exposure incidents.
This annual report summarizes Polish salmonellosis notifications, outbreaks, and laboratory limitations without presenting a reusable adjustment method.
The model-based cost-effectiveness analysis simulates meningococcal B vaccination under official and underreporting-corrected incidence scenarios with extensive sensitivity analysis.
The national survey describes blood-exposure circumstances and reporting barriers among young physicians without evaluating a correction or reporting intervention.
The record audit applies clinical pertussis criteria to primary-care cough visits and quantifies distinct underdetection and underreporting processes and their predictors.
The study uses Bayesian reclassification under alternative missed-case assumptions to correct pertussis case-definition misclassification in vaccine-effectiveness estimates.
The small passive tick survey reports local Borrelia prevalence and suspected establishment without developing a transferable surveillance-analysis method.

The ecological study uses lagged exposures and a multivariable Poisson model to impute missing national cancer incidence, a design transferable to infectious-burden estimation where country data are absent.
The study compares diagnosis-code counts, predictive logistic models, and laboratory-attributable cough time-series models as alternative methods for estimating underdetected pertussis incidence.
The study fits a stochastic transmission model to MERS surveillance and imported cases to separate zoonotic and human transmission and estimate spatially varying underreporting.
The systematic review characterizes the structure, assumptions, and policy uses of dynamic measles and rubella transmission models to guide future modeling.
The paper summarizes Japanese meningococcal notifications and serogroups after a reporting change without quantitatively evaluating a reusable surveillance method.
The study combines maximum-entropy ecological niche modeling and spatiotemporal scan statistics to identify avian-influenza risk areas, clusters, and possible underreporting.
The paper develops and tests a Bayesian stochastic SEIR framework with spline-based time-varying transmission that remains robust to aggregated data, uncertain initial states, and underreporting.
The study uses epidemic simulations to quantify contact-diary biases and develops surrogate-network reconstruction methods that recover risks obtained from sensor data.
The study uses insurance claims, diagnostic codes, treatment information, matched controls, and spatiotemporal analysis to estimate clinically recognized toxoplasmosis beyond conventional reporting.
The study develops catalytic-model inference from age-stratified dengue notifications, compares estimates with seroprevalence, and jointly estimates transmission intensity and reporting probability.
This short communication discusses yellow-fever resurgence and vaccine shortages without sufficient methodological analysis.
The study concerns stigmatization and self-reporting of illicit drug use and does not specify a concrete infectious-disease outcome or transfer route.

The multicenter study uses sampled record review, active case finding, trained abstractors, and expert adjudication to measure failures of passive adverse-event reporting, a design transferable to infection surveillance.
The study extracts primary-care and out-of-hours electronic records to estimate erythema migrans incidence that is absent from mandatory later-stage Lyme surveillance.
The study compares matched national surgical databases to assess the reliability of reported infection, neurologic injury, and mortality outcomes and investigates potential reporting confounding.
The analysis uses active trial surveillance to quantify how alternative dengue case definitions alter incidence and derives expansion factors against national passive systems.
The systematic review summarizes fragmented brucellosis frequencies and risk factors but does not substantively compare or prescribe evidence-synthesis or estimation methods.
Repeated WHO facility surveys measure HIV diagnostic and monitoring instrument capacity, utilization, downtime, and reporting completeness across countries.
The paper develops an underreporting correction factor that combines self-report with a specific biomarker, derives confidence intervals, and evaluates sensitivity to assumption violations.
The study surveys incompletely vaccinated students and cross-checks parent-reported general-practice doses against the national HPV register to identify missed doses and notification failures.
The review focuses on interpreting nutritional biomarkers during inflammation and does not provide a concrete method for an infectious-disease epidemiology outcome.
This brief neonatal listeriosis case-based discussion asserts underdiagnosis but supplies no transferable ascertainment or correction method.
The spatial reanalysis of a randomized pneumococcal-vaccine trial demonstrates distance-dependent outcome detection bias that can distort conventional vaccine-efficacy estimates.
The retrospective report estimates recent vaccination among febrile-convulsion cases but has largely missing exposure histories and offers no validated correction strategy.
The study develops maternal-mortality ascertainment for predominantly obstetric outcomes without identifying a concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The machine-learning method predicts pathogenic human genetic variants and has no stated infectious-disease epidemiology application or transfer route.
The study links laboratory and national chlamydia records, quantifies provider-specific underreporting and missing fields, and proposes a simplified laboratory-led surveillance design.
Qualitative reinterviews diagnose social, relational, confidentiality, survey-length, and recall influences on HIV risk self-reports and recommend methods to reduce response bias.
The study surveys affected farms and matches reported vaccine-associated bovine pancytopenia cases to a national pharmacovigilance database to quantify underreporting and incidence.
The paper describes economic consequences and reporting gaps for orbiviral livestock diseases without specifying a reusable burden-estimation or policy-evaluation method.
This eight-case melioidosis series describes presentations, risk factors, and outcomes without a transferable epidemiologic method.
The paper develops and simulation-validates a method for estimating transmission distance from onset times, generation intervals, and case locations despite severe underreporting.
The title indicates an influenza-outbreak underreporting study, but the absent abstract leaves its design and methodological contribution insufficiently specified.
The study constructs an underreporting-adjusted counterfactual to estimate polio cases, deaths, DALYs, and economic productivity gains attributable to India's elimination program.
The outbreak evaluation compares case-isolation delays and epidemiologic-link ascertainment before and after village-wide active surveillance and health education.
The paper develops approximation-based inference for underreported stochastic epidemics and compares its computational cost and accuracy with reversible-jump MCMC.
The study uses an underreporting-adjusted Markov-chain Monte Carlo simulation to project hepatitis B cases prevented by an Indigenous adult immunization program.
The review summarizes drug-resistant tuberculosis patterns and laboratory-capacity gaps in the Middle East without developing or comparing an epidemiologic method.

The review analyzes culture, recovery, infectivity, aggregation, and PCR-inhibition problems that bias pathogen quantification in sediments and explains their implications for source attribution and microbial risk assessment.
This study predicts general postoperative morbidity from renal insufficiency and does not establish a concrete infectious-disease epidemiology transfer route.
This annual surveillance report describes Polish salmonellosis notifications and probable underdiagnosis without applying a quantitative correction or validation method.
The study combines repeated transect sampling, trapping, fly dissection, infection testing, and spatial ecological interpretation to characterize trypanosomiasis vector risk.
The paper develops and simulation-tests a combined time-varying reproduction-number and gravity-dispersion model for separating local, imported, and cross-border Ebola transmission.
The study links congenital-syphilis notifications with mortality records to recover unreported fetal and infant deaths and quantify cause-specific underreporting.
The prospective occupational-injury report describes self-reported needlesticks and serologic follow-up without evaluating a reusable surveillance or reporting method.

The review combines published cases and postmarketing data, diagnoses inconsistent neuropsychiatric adverse-event terminology, and recommends standardized scales to improve antibiotic-safety ascertainment.
The review summarizes tuberculosis epidemiology, host factors, and drug resistance without developing or evaluating a transferable method.
The study compares weekly active seroepidemiologic surveillance with passive dengue reporting, derives an expansion factor, and propagates uncertainty into national DALY estimates.
The review narrates yellow-fever outbreaks, vaccination gaps, and risk factors without a substantive comparative or prescriptive methods contribution.
The questionnaire describes occupational exposures, reporting, and prophylaxis among medical students without testing a reporting or correction strategy.
The review summarizes Legionnaires' disease microbiology, diagnosis, and epidemiology without substantively comparing or developing methods.
The retrospective study reports routine spatial, temporal, and farming-sector associations for lumpy skin disease without a distinct reusable modeling or correction contribution.
The study actively reviews pediatric practice records and benchmarks identified adverse events following immunization against passive national reports to quantify underreporting.
The study uses a dynamic dengue-transmission model to compare vaccination programs, health effects, and value-based threshold prices under alternative reporting assumptions.
This case report and case-series review describes imported onchocerciasis manifestations and treatment without a transferable epidemiologic method.
The retrospective report describes rubella-positive febrile-rash cases and surveillance weaknesses without a reusable burden-correction or validation method.
The clinical-record review describes patients receiving HIV care and possible risk-behavior underreporting without validating a measurement or surveillance method.
The review focuses on causes and consequences of dengue underreporting in India and prescribes remedies spanning clinics, surveillance systems, and communities.
The report concerns sterile inflammatory-bowel-disease-associated abscesses and provides no concrete infectious-disease epidemiology transfer route.
The national laboratory audit compares stool-acceptance criteria and detection algorithms and shows how selective Giardia testing produces surveillance underreporting.
The study uses phosphatidylethanol as an objective validator of denied alcohol use in HIV care and identifies characteristics associated with self-report misclassification.

The study validates UNAIDS-modeled mortality against sibling histories, household deaths, vital registration, and orphanhood data and identifies systematic discrepancies in levels, ages, and sex ratios.
The questionnaire describes knowledge and practices concerning rickettsioses and Q fever without a reusable surveillance-measurement or analytic contribution.
The study links national registries and models influenza infection and vaccination as time-varying exposures to assess subsequent narcolepsy and hypersomnia.
The systematic case review links fumarate-associated progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy to lymphocytopenia and prescribes laboratory and symptom monitoring to reduce missed cases.
The article advocates innovative financing for rabies control but does not develop or evaluate an epidemiologic method.
This single osteomyelitis case illustrates bacterial misidentification but does not validate or develop a general diagnostic method.
The study combines HIV pol sequencing, large reference phylogenies, and donor interviews to infer transmission routes and quantify underreported male-to-male sex.
The evaluation combines stakeholder surveys, immunization-register coverage, and adverse-event reports to assess consistency, delivery, and data quality across BCG programs.
The stochastic simulation quantifies the probability that imported porcine plasma introduces epidemic-diarrhea virus and evaluates testing as a risk-mitigation strategy.
The matched outbreak case-control study combines individual risk-factor analysis with targeted environmental sampling to identify cholera transmission routes.
The study compares quantitative phosphatidylethanol with repeated self-reported drinking measures to identify and characterize alcohol underreporting among women with HIV.

The study evaluates birth and death record completeness, applies correction factors, and shows why race-specific underenumeration must be addressed when estimating mortality disparities, including infectious causes.

The paper synthesizes surveillance, literature, ministry analyses, and expert evidence into a national STI strategy specifying active case finding, targeted screening, and surveillance capacity improvements.
The survey evaluates local Chikungunya preparedness and response across operational domains and diagnoses low surveillance acceptability and flexibility during pathogen introduction.
The study measures social-desirability bias alongside computer-assisted HIV-risk reports and identifies behavior-, partner-, and gender-specific patterns of probable misreporting.
The study systematically synthesizes seroprevalence, primary-care, hospitalization, and mortality evidence and develops severity-stratified age-specific varicella burden estimates across Europe.
The validation study re-ascertains surgical-site infections and denominator fields to quantify underreporting caused by case-definition interpretation and case-finding variation.
The review summarizes associations between tobacco-smoke exposure and childhood respiratory outcomes without substantively comparing or developing epidemiologic methods.
The cohort separately follows indeterminate and negative HSV-2 serology and demonstrates how classification of indeterminate results materially changes incidence estimates.
The study benchmarks two conflict-zone cholera surveillance systems on timeliness, completeness, diagnostic testing, and case rates to expose systematic government underreporting.

The study probabilistically links tuberculosis and AIDS notification databases to recover missed coinfections, quantify bidirectional underreporting, and recommend periodic linkage as a correction procedure.
